elements.name: Title
texts: "When any Superstitious Place is defaced or demolished, they repair it"

elements.name: Description
texts: A critique of St. Patrick's Purgatory and Catholic pilgrimage in general.

elements.name: Source
texts: The Great Folly, superstition and idolatry of Pilgrimages in Ireland, especialy of that to St. Patrick's Purgatory: together with an account of the loss that the publick sustaineth thereby, truly and impartially represented, Preface

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Collections numérisées - Université de Rennes 2

elements.name: Text
texts: "When any Superstitious Place is defaced or demolished, they repair it, and seem to be more inclined to Resort to it than formerly. They account it Meritorious to adhere obstinately to a Practice prohibited by Hereticks, and if any Punishment be inflicted upon them for it, they believe they suffer for Righteousness sake."
